$comment: keemakr Marketplace submission for the kee-sterling-finance agent. This is the EntrySubmission the platform's registerEntry() ingests (see keemakr-core src/lib/marketplace/types.ts). Installing it for a tenant creates the 'finance' department + its agents, gated on the one required connection (Stripe) below.
description: Monitors revenue and flags churn and failed-payment risk. Give it an account's billing snapshot — MRR, days overdue, failed charges, plan — and it returns a deterministic risk level, a recommended action, and a ready-to-send dunning draft.
author: keemakr
overview: Sterling Finance watches your billing health and turns raw numbers into a clear next step. From an account's MRR, overdue days, and failed charges it scores churn and payment-failure risk on a deterministic low / watch / high / critical scale. For each at-risk account it recommends an action and drafts the dunning outreach for you. It is the smallest focused finance entry that still exercises the platform's full register, resolve-dependencies, and install round-trip.
operator: Powered by the EVE Finance operator runtime — the keemakr operator hydrates each account's billing snapshot from your connected Stripe data and delegates the assessment to this remote eve agent over HTTP.
usage: 1. Connect your Stripe account so the operator can read charges, subscriptions, and payment failures. 2. Install the entry — the Finance department and its Revenue Monitor and Payment Recovery agents are seeded for your tenant. 3. The Revenue Monitor watches billing events and calls assess_account to flag at-risk accounts. 4. Review each flagged account's recommended action and edit-then-send the drafted dunning outreach.
changelog: Initial release — revenue monitoring + churn / failed-payment risk assessment.

agents.description: Watches billing events and flags risk via assess_account.
Drafts dunning outreach for failed payments.

dependencies.reason: Read charges, subscriptions, and payment failures.
